steps.title: Identify what 'therefore' must explain
steps.content: The passage contrasts two studies' results—Welles found right-handedness in captive animals; McGrew found no such tendency in wild animals—and identifies human contact as the only distinguishing factor mentioned (“captive animals spend significantly more time with humans than wild animals do, and therefore”). The key signal is that the passage has explicitly named human contact as what must explain the difference.

steps.title: Match the cause of the difference
steps.content: B proposes that extended human contact might cause the captive animals' right-handedness. A, C, and D all introduce explanations the passage never mentions—methodology differences, sample size, and sample size. That matches choice B — B is correct.
